title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run over 4.1 innings in the Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cardinals.
Early struggled with command, issuing two walks and hitting a batter, leading to a high pitch count.
Reliever Zack Kelly gave up the lead in the fifth, contributing to the Red Sox's 4-9 season record.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta has played 75 games for the Celtics, starting in 74, averaging 25.5 minutes per game.
Queta recorded his 100th block of the season and has achieved 17 double-doubles, highlighting his defensive impact.
The Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs following their game against the Pelicans.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox's top four batters produced one hit Friday, struggling against pitcher Dustin May.
Wilyer Abreu and Roman Anthony suggested for Red Sox's 4th and 5th lineup spots to boost production.
Masataka Yoshida and Ceddanne Rafaela considered for leadoff position to enhance early scoring opportunities.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 144-118 win over the Pelicans.
Celtics tied the NBA single-game three-point record with 29 threes against New Orleans.
Boston will face the winner of the Magic vs. 76ers play-in tournament in the playoffs.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Pizza Movie' starring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone is available on Hulu.
'The Boys' Season 5 featuring Antony Starr is streaming on Prime Video.
'The Testaments' based on Margaret Atwood's story is streaming on Hulu.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will not play Friday against the Pelicans due to right Achilles management.
Jaylen Brown is available for Friday's game despite ongoing Achilles tendinitis issues.
Three other Celtics starters are probable but expected to play against the Pelicans.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's cancer journey.
Robertson's 16-year-old daughter, Emma, was diagnosed with a medulloblastoma that metastasized, leading to intensive treatments.
Robertson hopes to raise awareness and support for Emma's medical team at Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not make his NHL debut for the Bruins against the Tampa Bay Lightning on Saturday.
Coach Marco Sturm is exercising caution with Hagens, focusing on his acclimation to the team's system.
The Bruins seek a win over Tampa to clinch a playoff spot after a one-year absence.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes' lists of 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' include Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers.
The historic list also features figures with local ties, such as Benjamin Franklin, Babe Ruth, and Louisa May Alcott.
Rankings are based on 'Self-Made Scores' assessing financial success, obstacles overcome, and the individual's long-term impact.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon takes place on April 20, with 30,000 runners from over 130 countries and all 50 U.S. states.
New this year, the marathon will feature six start waves instead of four, with the first wave beginning at 10 a.m.
The Boston Marathon's course starts in Hopkinton, passing through several towns before finishing at Copley Square in Boston.
